text.title: Weighted Decision Matrix Core Rules
text.summary: 7 testable rules grounding the trade-off-decision-matrix methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale; the final rule is the skip-this-methodology gate referenced by the decision tree.

rule.statement: Criteria weights MUST sum to exactly 100% and MUST be non-zero. Weights MUST be set by named stakeholders, not the architect alone.
rule.rationale: Un-summed weights silently bias the result; architect-only weighting hides value calls from their rightful owners.

rule.statement: Every score (1-5) MUST cite at least one piece of evidence: benchmark number, case study link, or POC observation. Intuition-only scores MUST be rejected.
rule.rationale: Intuition scoring rationalises a pre-decided answer; evidence forces honest comparison.

rule.statement: Matrix MUST name at least one cross-criterion conflict (e.g. cost vs performance, security vs usability) and document a resolution rule.
rule.rationale: Unnamed conflicts surface as ad-hoc decisions later; named conflicts produce stable trade-off rules.

rule.statement: When two options finish within ≤5% of each other, the matrix MUST trigger a documented tie-break rule (team familiarity, reversibility, faster POC) — gut-feel tie-break MUST NOT be used.
rule.rationale: Close calls otherwise default to bias; an explicit rule produces audit-defensible choice.

rule.statement: Matrix MUST be version-controlled with the ADR, MUST include a review date, and MUST be re-run if criteria or weights change > 10%.
rule.rationale: Stale matrices justify outdated decisions; version control + review preserve auditability.

rule.statement: When prerequisites hold (shortlist, stakeholders, evidence, question), apply r1 → r5 and emit the matrix + recommendation.
rule.rationale: Order encodes risk: weights first, evidence second, conflicts third, tie-break fourth, version control last.

rule.statement: Do NOT use when (a) single option, (b) hard constraint dominates, (c) trivial reversible choice. Exit with note.
rule.rationale: Matrix theatre in these contexts wastes review time.
